heading: Critical document fee waiver
label: 5174b Critical document fee waiver

DefinitionsFor definitions of terms used in this section, see , set out as a note under .section 1203 of Pub. L. 115–254section 5122 of this title

Delegation of Functions and Authorities Under Section 1238 of the FAA Reauthorization Act of 2018Memorandum of President of the United States, , 84 F.R. 3957, provided:Dec. 21, 2018Memorandum for the Secretary of State [and] the Secretary of Homeland SecurityBy the authority vested in me as President by the Constitution and the laws of the United States of America, including , United States Code, I hereby:section 301 of title 3(1) delegate to the Secretary of State the functions and authorities vested in the President by sections 1238(a)(1)(A)–(B) of the FAA Reauthorization Act of 2018 () [–(B)]; andPublic Law 115–25442 U.S.C. 5174b(a)(1)(A)(2) delegate to the Secretary of Homeland Security the functions and authorities vested in the President by sections 1238(a)(1)(C)–(H) of the FAA Reauthorization Act of 2018.The delegations in this memorandum shall apply to any provisions of any future public law that are the same or substantially the same as the provisions referenced in this memorandum. The Secretary of State and the Secretary of Homeland Security may redelegate within their departments the functions and authorities delegated by this memorandum to the extent authorized by law.The Secretary of State is authorized and directed to publish this memorandum in the Federal Register.Donald J. Trump.

Delegation of Functions and Authorities Under Section 1238 of the FAA Reauthorization Act of 2018Memorandum of President of the United States, , 84 F.R. 3957, provided:Dec. 21, 2018Memorandum for the Secretary of State [and] the Secretary of Homeland SecurityBy the authority vested in me as President by the Constitution and the laws of the United States of America, including , United States Code, I hereby:section 301 of title 3(1) delegate to the Secretary of State the functions and authorities vested in the President by sections 1238(a)(1)(A)–(B) of the FAA Reauthorization Act of 2018 () [–(B)]; andPublic Law 115–25442 U.S.C. 5174b(a)(1)(A)(2) delegate to the Secretary of Homeland Security the functions and authorities vested in the President by sections 1238(a)(1)(C)–(H) of the FAA Reauthorization Act of 2018.The delegations in this memorandum shall apply to any provisions of any future public law that are the same or substantially the same as the provisions referenced in this memorandum. The Secretary of State and the Secretary of Homeland Security may redelegate within their departments the functions and authorities delegated by this memorandum to the extent authorized by law.The Secretary of State is authorized and directed to publish this memorandum in the Federal Register.Donald J. Trump.

children.label: A
children.text: The passport application fee for individuals who lost their United States passport in a major disaster within the preceding three calendar years.

children.label: B
children.text: The file search fee for a United States passport.

children.label: C
children.text: The Application for Waiver of Passport and/or Visa form (Form I–193) fee.

children.label: D
children.text: The Permanent Resident Card replacement form (Form I–90) filing fee.

children.label: E
children.text: The Declaration of Intention form (Form N–300) filing fee.

children.label: F
children.text: The Naturalization/Citizenship Document replacement form (Form N–565) filing fee.

children.label: G
children.text: The Employment Authorization form (Form I–765) filing fee.

children.heading: Exemption from form requirement
children.label: 2 Exemption from form requirement
children.text: The authority of the President to waive fees under subparagraphs (C) through (H) of paragraph (1) applies regardless of whether the individual or household qualifies for a Form I–912 Request for Fee Waiver, or any successor thereto.

children.heading: Exemption from assistance maximum
children.label: 3 Exemption from assistance maximum
children.text: The assistance limit in shall not apply to any fee waived under this subsection.section 5174(h) of this title

children.heading: Report
children.label: 4 Report
children.text: Not later than 365 days after , the Administrator and the head of any other agency given critical document fee waiver authority under this subsection shall submit a report to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives on the costs associated with providing critical document fee waivers as described in paragraph (1).October 5, 2018
